summary: Validated server-side KYC gate, password flows, and frontend KYC-required UX. 21/21 new KYC-gate pytest cases pass; 57/64 existing CASP regression pass (7 pre-existing test-assertion mismatches unrelated to KYC gate). Frontend: Retail Ramp CTAs correctly opacity-60 and link to /onboarding when KYC not APPROVED; axios 'neonoble:kyc-required' event fires sonner toast and auto-redirects to /onboarding in ~1.5s.

minor.issue: Returns 400 'Unable to fetch price for BTC' intermittently — upstream pricing service flakiness. Not a KYC-gate issue (gate bypass for ADMIN confirmed working).

test_casp.py expects ≥7 KPI, ≥1 KYB BigCorp, ≥7 wallets, ≥5 OTC, ≥4 disclosures, ≥1 complaint — actual seed counts are lower (1, 0, 1, 3, 0 resp.). Pre-existing seeding mismatch in test_casp.py assertions, unrelated to this iteration's KYC-gate work.

Existing test_casp.py::test_transak_widget_url asserts status in (200,400,422,502) but endpoint returns 409 TRANSAK_KYB_PENDING which is the documented expected state. Update assertion to include 409.

action_items: (Optional) Update /app/backend/tests/test_casp.py to (a) accept 409 for transak/widget-url and (b) relax seed-count assertions to match current seed (or re-seed CASP fixtures to expected counts).
(Optional) Investigate upstream CoinGecko BTC pricing intermittency surfaced by /api/ramp/onramp/quote — add retry/fallback in pricing service.
critical_code_review_comments: kyc_gate.py correctly distinguishes 401 (no/invalid token) from 403 (kyc_required) — verified via 5/5 unauthenticated parametrized tests returning 401.
ADMIN/DEVELOPER bypass at middleware/kyc_gate.py:35 confirmed (admin onramp/quote does NOT return 403).
Structured 403 detail (`error`, `kyc_status`, `message`) is consistent across all 5 gated endpoints — required by frontend axios interceptor.
Password reset flow is properly single-use (jti cleared after consumption) and old-password rejection verified after reset.
Frontend axios interceptor → CustomEvent → App.js handler → sonner toast + navigate('/onboarding') chain works end-to-end.

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 new KYC-gate); 89% (57/64 regression — pre-existing test-data drift)

test_credentials: Seeded MLRO/admin: casp-admin@neonoble.example.com / casp-mlro@neonoble.example.com — password CaspAdmin!2026. Ephemeral users TEST_<uuid>@example.com / TestPass123!.
seed_data_creation: Tests register ephemeral TEST_* users on the fly; KYC records start via /api/onboarding/kyc/start; MLRO approves via /api/casp/kyc/{id}/decision. No persistent seed data needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: KYC gate is fully wired and verified. If retesting, /app/backend/tests/test_kyc_gate.py covers the entire matrix (5 endpoints × {401, 403 not-started, 403 in-review, 200/409 after approve}, admin bypass, password reset cycle, change-password). The 7 pre-existing test_casp.py failures are unrelated seed-count assertion drift — investigate only if main agent wants to clean up that file.
rca of the issue: N/A — all reported KYC-gate behaviors confirmed working as designed. Pre-existing test_casp.py drift is a test-assertion / seed-count mismatch, not a product defect.
